purpose: Test one final development-train-only marginal pair-synergy QUBO after raw aggregation-aligned pair utility failed to improve over classical baselines.

protocol_amendment.negative_finding: Aggregation-aligned raw pair BEDROC improved over active overlap but the selected QUBO duplicated its matched linear subset and remained below matched linear and nested greedy BEDROC.
protocol_amendment.distinct_hypothesis: A pair should receive quadratic credit only for BEDROC beyond its stronger singleton. For each aggregation and current training fold, S_ij = BEDROC(i,j) - max(BEDROC(i), BEDROC(j)).
protocol_amendment.signed_scaling: Divide every raw S_ij in the current training fold by the maximum absolute pair synergy in that fold. This preserves negative, zero, and positive synergy; an all-zero map remains all zero.
protocol_amendment.scope_control: Use singleton BEDROC as the linear utility, aggregation-aligned signed marginal pair synergy as the only performance quadratic term, inherited singleton seed stability, and the cardinality penalty. All coverage, decoy exposure, active overlap, redundancy, and raw pair-utility weights are fixed to zero.
protocol_amendment.stopping_rule: Run the frozen gate twice to verify deterministic equality. Record pass or failure, then stop Train-696 QUBO objective and weight tuning for this study phase.
forbidden_actions: change the 24-candidate grid after observing results
clip negative pair synergy or min-max shift it into a positive reward
reuse a held-out fold when calculating singleton or pair BEDROC

cross_validation.normalization: fit independent per-receptor min-max bounds on each matrix and training fold only
cross_validation.pair_synergy_fit_scope: calculate singleton BEDROC and min-score or mean-score pair BEDROC strictly inside each current training fold
tuning_order: maximize worst individual-seed mean inner-fold BEDROC
maximize primary median-matrix mean inner-fold BEDROC
prefer smaller synergy weight, smaller stability weight, and deterministic lexical order

model.pair_synergy_definition: signed max-absolute-scaled BEDROC(i,j)-max(BEDROC(i),BEDROC(j)), matched to candidate aggregation and fitted within the current training fold
model.seed_stability_definition: negative population standard deviation of normalized singleton BEDROC across seed0, seed1, and seed2; min-max scaled so more stable receptors receive larger rewards
model.candidate_filter: Every candidate has positive ensemble_pair_synergy, zero raw pair utility, and zero other non-cardinality quadratic weights.

interpretation_boundary: This final post-negative development-train-only method gate tests marginal pair synergy. A pass would nominate but not validate a QUBO candidate. A failure ends Train-696 objective tuning in this study phase. Validation/test remain unavailable, and no result establishes affinity, activity, cross-target generalization, or quantum advantage.
